title: Decide lifecycle for legacy CBOR native protocol surfaces
description: Follow-up from project-LICHEN-py4c.1.13. The spec/lichen-native directory and matching Python/C native framing libraries are now marked historical and non-authoritative for LCI, but they remain in tree for prototype compatibility and tests. Decide whether to keep them as legacy compatibility, rename them to avoid 'native' ambiguity, gate them behind legacy Kconfig/package names, or remove them once all users migrate to IPv6 + CoAP LCI.
design:
Decision: keep the legacy CBOR native protocol surfaces only as historical prototype compatibility for existing tests, simulators, board configs, and archived tooling. They are not LCI, not the native app contract, and not a surface for new BLE, USB, serial, IP, firmware, simulator, or Python-native client features.

Authoritative native app contract: IPv6 + CoAP LCI in spec/11-lci.md. BLE LCI uses SLIP-framed IPv6 over a BLE UART-like service as specified there; UUID/profile selection remains governed by spec/11-lci.md and the BLE owner docs, not by the historical CBOR native protocol. Raw diagnostics use /diag/raw/* CoAP resources only; clients must not fall back to CBOR raw_tx/raw_rx/raw_rx_enable.

Remaining legacy surfaces: spec/lichen-native remains as canonical historical reference; python/src/lichen/interface remains legacy CBOR prototype compatibility; lichen/lib/native and CONFIG_LICHEN_NATIVE remain for existing Zephyr tests and board configs; lichen,native-uart remains until a compatibility-preserving HAL/devicetree rename is implemented.

Compatibility guarantee: legacy CBOR framing is frozen and retained for prototype compatibility only. It has no forward feature guarantee; new capabilities belong in LCI CoAP resources or separate compatibility protocols. Removing it now would break python/tests/interface/*, Zephyr native_init/native_parse tests, prototype TCP/serial bindings, and T1000-E/R1 Neo board configs.

Migration plan: README now describes LICHEN Native LCI as IPv6+CoAP instead of CBOR. Follow-up Beads cover Python legacy naming/gating (project-LICHEN-py4c.1.18.2), Zephyr legacy CBOR naming/gating (project-LICHEN-py4c.1.18.1), and tighter spec/lichen-native historical wording (project-LICHEN-py4c.1.18.3).
acceptance_criteria: Decision records which legacy CBOR surfaces remain, which are renamed/removed/gated, migration impact for tests and simulator/prototype tooling, and any compatibility guarantees. If implementation is chosen, child Beads cover Python and Zephyr changes with no-hardware tests.
notes:
Mapped legacy CBOR native surfaces with explorer agents. README had the only directly conflicting top-level user-facing wording; updated it to identify LICHEN Native LCI as IPv6+CoAP and retain spec/lichen-native only for historical prototype compatibility. Added python/tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py guard against the stale CBOR README wording. Targeted checks: python/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q python/tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py -> 8 passed in 0.03s; python/.venv/bin/python -m ruff check python/tests/test_lci_contract_docs.py -> All checks passed; git diff --check -> passed. No EC2 needed for README/test decision work; Zephyr implementation deferred to child Bead project-LICHEN-py4c.1.18.1.
Review follow-up: Cicero the 9th found the first decision wording over-specified BLE UUID/NUS policy relative to spec/11-lci.md. Fixed the parent decision to defer BLE UUID/profile selection to spec/11-lci.md and BLE owner docs while preserving the legacy-CBOR lifecycle decision.
close_reason: Completed: lifecycle decision recorded; README corrected; docs contract test added; Python, Zephyr, and spec follow-up Beads created.
